data.title: Add Auto-Resume Detection section with WORKTREE_TRACKING scan to fix-bug/SKILL.md after Step 0.5
data.description:
## Story
930f-8991 — Auto-detect and recover single abandoned worktree branch on resume

## testing_mode
GREEN

## What
Modify `plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md` to add an **Auto-Resume Detection** sub-section after Step 0.5 (after ticket is set to in_progress).

Add immediately after the in_progress transition in Step 0.5:

```
#### Auto-Resume Detection

After transitioning the bug ticket to in_progress, scan for abandoned worktrees from prior sessions:

1. Read comments on the bug ticket for WORKTREE_TRACKING:start entries with no corresponding :complete
2. For each unmatched start, extract the branch:
   - If branch no longer exists: skip without error
   - If branch is ancestor of HEAD: write retroactive :complete with outcome=already_merged
   - If mid-merge state (MERGE_HEAD exists): run `git merge --abort` first
   - If branch has unique commits: attempt `git merge --no-edit <branch>`
     - Success: log 'Merged abandoned branch <b>'
     - Conflict: run `git merge --abort`, log 'Conflict in <b> — discarded'
3. Proceed with normal fix-bug flow

Note: debug-everything's Bug-Fix Mode dispatches bug fix sub-agents with worktree isolation but does not include this scan; document as a follow-up if observed in practice.
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 6720-39b0 structural boundary tests pass GREEN for fix-bug/SKILL.md
- [ ] fix-bug/SKILL.md contains 'Auto-Resume Detection' heading near Step 0.5
- [ ] WORKTREE_TRACKING scan instructions present: covers unmatched-start logic, branch-not-found, ancestor, mid-merge abort, merge attempt, conflict discard
